channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-19 14:17 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Early replies (first 3)

62382825
never. Its not about the link token, its just about the hope for the future it gives. That is what keeps me going, even if its a delusion. Thus I will never sell, because as long as I hold the token that fantastical delusion can still be real in my mind

62382842
chatgpt post

62382990
>>62382842
OP is a mentally ill faggot

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62383192
>>62382813
Do one for spacex for us now please
Ahahahahahhaha

62383251
>>62382842
>>62382990
This

62383254
>>62382813
Is it a good time to buy yet or not? We have been trying to decide for years

62383255
>>62383132
Overused buzzwords, here a few more
>the price is bad because they are supressing it not because i picked an underperforming asset
>lol it's a cult of course i ignore any rationale that goes against sergey
>staking is already live
>ok yeah the fees are going to cll instead of stakers but they're doing buy backs
>sure they dump 17 million tokens and rebuy 100k of them, but buybacks man
>yeah swift and dtcc are going live without the "necessary" decentralized oracle network, but they will totally need it later on for some reason

62383260
I want a linkmarine to ram my pussy

62383263
>>62383255
Post your holdings and your hand

62383265
I'm selling in 2028/2029. I still remember back in 2021 when LINK was $45 and I kept debating with myself whether to sell or not and decided not to. That shit is going to haunt me for the rest of my life.

62383267
>>62383255
>>the price is bad because they are supressing it
yes, see pic

>>sure they dump 17 million tokens and rebuy 100k of them
We've known about the supply unlocks for 9 years, we've known about the buybacks for less than a year

>swift and dtcc are going live without the "necessary" decentralized oracle network
Every single Chainlink product uses the decentralized oracle network. What a bizarre thing to just lie about.

62383269
>>62383263
>no i totally don't hold the token, i just know everything about it to more accuratelly fud
*cries in third world*

62383274
>>62383267
>the switch bro's, the switch is flipped
>surely the price must be pushing higher from all these onchain buy fees towards the link token
>wait
>why is everything a cash deal between that company and cll
>why is cll paying their own nodes

62383275
>>62383274
>>why is cll paying their own nodes
Because cll runs the contract.

Holy shit you're retarded

62383279
>>62383275
You see this lurkers? Another layer of cope comes forth.

62383287
>>62383279
I love how you always pretend you have this huge silent audience. This board is dead, maybe 15 people will ever see your posts, and 14 of them will instantly know you're a clueless retard.

62383291
>>62383287
I love how you always pretend you have this answer for brownian motion. This board is an echo chamber, maybe 15 people will ever see your posts, and 14 of them will instantly know you're the same as them.

62383298
>>62383291
>same as them
>same skin color as them
Sorry, I want to add that to the end of my post. Makes it way funnier.

62383299
Ram my pussy bitches

62383312
>>62383299
Sorry femanon, the marines have been beating their dicks senseless to anime jpegs for years. I don't think they can keep it up for a real woman. They'll probably cum too fast and watch some bull finish you.

62383932
>>62382813
I'v been waiting for SWIFT, DTCC, and the whole banking system to come online with tokenization using Chainlink, just like everyone else has. And we're very close, anon. Can you smell it? Smell it.

62387308
>>62383932
I can smell your poverty kek

62387313
>>62383312
Now this is some real fuddie cuck fanfiction. Bravo.

62387314
>>62387313
Link marines are unironic cuckolds who get off seeing Sergey dump hundreds of millions of tokens on Binance. They've been waiting 9 years for their "real life" to begin kek

62387379
>>62383932
This. Just 2 more years and we will all make it

62387714
>>62382813
I've been holding since september or november 2017, bought at 30c average sold 2/3 of my original stack between $38 and $12, still holding the rest, but there is nobody I despise more than idiots defending "the team" and coping, I know many of you retards bought at $5 or even higher and are sweating right now, and that's a good thing, you don't deserve anything better than the current price action. Instead of giving Chainlink the heat they deserve you idiots defend them. At least cocksuckers like that chainlinkgod retard or the catfishy catfish idiot get paid for doing damage control, but you absolute miserable fucks do it for free

62387730
>>62382825
the memories of defi summer are still fresh in my mind. we really had fun, didn't we

62387778
In all these years nobody could ever explain what Chainlink is even supposed to be doing without the explanation devolving into a buzzword ouroboros.

62387876
>>62382825
I just wish you would discuss your beanie babies somewhere else

62387900
>>62387778
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4hR4rDbJPM4

62387912
>>62387876
Stop making threads then

62387923
>>62387912
I have made as many threads about the topic as chainlink has intrinsic value. (0)

62387994
>>62387714
You sound pretty angry.

item.title: Uh? This will have consequences — 34 replies — 136.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Uh? This will have consequences

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-19 14:10 UTC

OP

https://www.usdebtclock.org/

Are you all just shit coin investors? Did any of you even bother to do the math on this?

Literally, the US is going to enter into debt insolvency at about 55 trillion. That will happen pretty fast.

What does this have to do with business?

Well, the US has lost HALF of it's money's value in the last 25 years. That is going to get exponentially worse.

I get it, twenty years ago, people reeeee'd about "meh hyper inflations"... but seriously, the hour glass is losing sand fast.
|
The public debt will become your problem eventually.

Early replies (first 3)

62384457
wrong

62384460
>>62384445
>This will have consequences
cope

62384462
wen doe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384542
>>62384477
underage b&

62384570
>>62384445
>big numbers I don't understand are scary!
the world was supposed to end in 2020 and 2012 too right?

62384584
>>62384460
>the empire will never collapse
>because it just wont ok
kek lmao even

62384611
>>62384460
Retard. I bet if you asked the Roman Empire back then if they thought they would ever collapse they would probably think you are insane. Prepare now.

62384703
>>62384570
if you believe a market correction is tantamount to "the end of the world" then it only goes to show how raped the economy truly is and that you've internalized this fact without even realizing

62384734
>>62384703
I didn't say anything about a market correction nigger, i'm trying to say people have been calling for the end of the world forever and it never happens. despite doomer niggers screeching the world has gotten better over time for the average person

62384788
>>62384734
Let's talk math. Given that the US must roll over a third of its near-zero interest debt to market rates this year, and given current trends in Federal spending and revenue, and given current interest rates, the interest on the debt ALONE will exceed all Federal revenue in about ten years. And that is a conservative estimate because it does not take into account waning interest in US treasury paper, the loss of revenue from the current recession, and the fact that interest rates will almost certainly begin climbing rapidly as the deadline approaches. So what then? There is no political will to make the drastic cuts in spending that would be needed to turn the ship around. When the USA starts paying the mortgage with a credit card, the MMT scam is finished. Not the end of the world, but the end of the American empire. That is for sure.

62384816
>>62384788
I’ve been hearing this for 20 years

62384818
>>62384445
>1pbtid
so sad

62384829
>>62384445
>the large amount of existing dollars will have consequences
???
if this number was 0 it'd mean that nobody is using dollars

62384972
>>62384734
>i'm trying to say people have been calling for the end of the world forever and it never happens.
The OP didn’t say anything about the world ending though

62385662
>>62384788
>blocks your path

62385676
>>62384445
>The public debt will become your problem eventually.
it wont
unless you keep bagholding fiat

62385763
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62385793
>>62384445
>Burgerland
Who gives a fuck about that shit hole country?

62386424
>>62384788
>There is no political will to make the drastic cuts in spending that would be needed to turn the ship around.
DeSantis and some friends are going around the country trying to convince state legislatures to pass a constitutional balanced budget amendment without Congress.
There's a surprising number of states that have already signed on.

62386723
>>62384445
Day traders and nepobabies don't care because they reckon it's a criminal's economy and nobody will ever do anything about it anymore.

Maybe they're right.

62386736
>>62384445
The debt is already insolvent.

62386773
>>62386424
I wish them luck. Seriously. But I'm betting it won't happen.

62386776
>>62385662
Now is not really the optimal moment for rattling the American saber, seeing as how a bunch of rag head are twisting Trump's nut sack with impunity. Really think they are going to bully the whole world into using toilet paper for money? Those days are over.

62386859
>>62384445
The solution is very simple and everyone knows it. You know what it is too.

62386860
>>62384445
just roll it forward

62386870
>>62386424
None of that means anything unless the core problem is addressed. Government spending is only an issue after that fact.

62387790
>>62384570
it did

62387805
i dont hear anyone complaining that the government doesnt pay them. ever thought about that? its meaningless. there is no creditor

62387808
you could set it to $0 and its effectively the same. in fact that's the plan

62387956
>>62387808
I think you should do this

62387969
>>62384818
im on mobile and my id constantly changes
i imagine this is most posters
just realize this is the world now

62387975
At least fort knox still has gold in it
Geg

item.title: My x feed has turn into this constantly. — 28 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
My x feed has turn into this constantly.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-19 12:27 UTC

OP

My x feed has turn into this constantly.

1) some "omfg" Claude workflow that will make you 60k/month
2) retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships in how to run a gazillion automated YT/tiktok/whatever pages with screenshots of students making money
3) various clickbait: "MrBeast shares key insights", "This guy just shared to how to make 10k a month with sleep channels", "this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"

It never ends. And now with AI the effort on having to actually write something catchy isn't there. It's just constant slop with supposed cheat codes to create slop that you can monetize.

The reality is this:
-The slop on the image makes no sense, you cannot replace a Bloomberg terminal with AI because a Bloomberg terminal gets emerging constant new sources that you don't have. Of course im not saying paying for a Bloomberg terminal is going to give you an edge and make you money. But just proving my point. Also all of these "free" workflows always have a paywall somewhere.
-Course selling scammers make money from the courses, this is not news.
-Clickbaiters make money from the traffic.

No one is making money doing something new and cool. By no one I mean, 99% of people. Everything is slop. Anyone making money is doing it from some sort of loophole. It's literally everywhere. There is always something dodgy going on.

For everything cool and genuine (like John Carmack inveting Doom in 1993 and getting rich because the game was just that good and revolutionary) 99% of people that get rich are doing it scaling slop. It will only get worse as AI gets trained on AI generated slop.

And if you try to profit from this, it's just algorithm luck to get your account ranked by the system so your posts get anywhere. Everything is literally a lottery, unless your genius is so good that it eventually thrives even if you are viewthrottled by the algorithm, but that is insanely rare.

Early replies (first 3)

62382855
>>62382838
Its always funny when people for these posts. Why the fuck would someone teach other people on ways of making easy money? Its like people selling trading bots

62382857
>>62382838
You should just stop using X. I rarely even visit this site anymore. Just start building something without all that crap bombarding your mental and you'll be better off. It's all just poison for your time.

62382861
>>62382857
There is no other place to go isn't it? I have another account that I use to find interesting tickers on semis as well as Bitcoin TA takes and basically get a news feed from the walter bloomberg guy. Everyone is on x, where the fuck do you go, facebook?
The thing is, 99% is slop, there's a few good accounts, but most is just monetized slop farms.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382867
maybe unsubscribe from slop?

62382873
>>62382838
top signal

62382878
>>62382867
you can't lol
my xitter exclusively follows gook artists because pixiv sucks but it still bombards me with what is basically ads from grifters

62382884
>>62382861
I don't go anywhere else it's all pretty much the same stuff really. I guess for "socials" I just lurk some sources I like on youtube but for the most part I'm doing my own thing and everything else is just noise. These social platforms like X are all bots and junk there isn't any real substance to any of it.

62382885
>>62382867
Im not subscribed. Even if you unfollow all these accounts, they still show up because they are accounts win tons of followers. Also there's basically no good accounts except such a few ammounts that the feed is just going to get these bigger accounts anyway. Unless you limit it all to the same few accounts you follow, but this way you close yourself to finding something new worth reading.

62382892
>>62382861
Go nowhere, put the time towards slowing down and doing nothing. Honest to god I don’t even miss YouTube anymore. My only social media is Twitch Discord and this place.

62382921
>>62382857
This. X is literally like visiting India. Here is like visiting a boomer's basement to see his millennial son.

62382933
>>62382838
Who cares.

62382980
>>62382884
So you only lurk here?
How is this place any better?
I mean sure, the fact that you cannot make money here is great, you get real people, and the bots have gone down by a lot with the new captcha it seems, but this is still a slop website

62382982
>>62382892
twitch, are we fucking serious? what do you even do there but watch livesloppers
and discord, what channels? I only chat in like 2 places

62383031
>retards selling courses/coaching/mentorships
If you are a retard and ask an LLM how to make money, it will recommend selling some type of online courses.

62383080
>>62382838
>"this guy just made $2,582,285 on a memecoin"
this one is the least believable kek
Crypto is so dead

62383308
Its like how people who buy target funds are exit liquidty for other investors and nations. The people that buy it are exit liquidity for scammers. Most scammers probably got scammed multiple times before they figured it out.

62383318
>>62382892
>Twitch
This anon pays other men to fuck his wife.

62384938
>>62382838
I-is picrel true? Is anthropic really publishing the inner workings of the financial system for free?

62384950
>>62383031
>"Claude make a course on how to make money, upload it and link it to my square account. Make it the best one"

62385508
>>62382982
>>62383318
Nta but I put twitch on in the background (with an adblocker) while watching streams that have a timer in full view and use that to time my sets for exercise (sometimes while I get high; that's when I want the giant timer)
I don't honestly give a fuck what's going on in the stream 99% of the time since modern games are shit I have no interest in following along and most streamers are manchildren throwing out the worst political 'hot takes' imaginable
Then I'll either mute it or turn it off when I'm done and meditate with stretching
If I put something on that's worth watching, it distracts from the exercise, ergo twitch fills the niche

62385516
>>62382838
A couple years ago I was getting ahead on twitter and my posts started getting stolen by bot accounts mimicking mine. Then I said something bad about israel and got shadow banned (went from +100-200 a week to 0)

62385681
>>62384950
It makes no fucking sense to me. How would you substitute a Bloomberg terminal without having constant access to a Bloomberg terminal to begin with?
Looks like these things are just a bunch of slop models put together and then sold with marketing (here the marketing is, we are getting data from Bloomberg). If the models are updated constantly, there is probably a paid subscription, and how do you even audit they are getting data from Bloomberg and integrating it correctly and it's not some random dude adding a bunch of stuff on a model and reselling it as comming from Bloomberg.

62385871
>>62382867
I always block them and then get reccomended the new ones

62385874
>>62382857
That's probably the best advice I've seen on this board.
Twitter/X is full of retards, shills, and bots.

62386368
Cool is subjective, but if you want effective and the best solution currently available, then

viewpoint214.com.

It's not a course or click bait. And would be of the 1% you seek out.

62386407
>>62382861
You can go to viewpoint214.com

It doesn't cost you any money. Check out the website it's not a scam or grifting...

62387648
>>62386368
>>62386407
wtf

62387678
>>62386407
>>62386368
Mods pls ban this jeet

item.title: BREAKING NEWS: GTA6 OFFICIALLY RUMORED TO BE USING XRP IN THE GAME TRANSACTIONS — 9 replies — 7.5/hr

62387234

XRP is hyper liquidity for banks. AKA their person piggy bank AKA for recovery slush fund issuing dodgy loans to niggers when they default.

62387235

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387255

no fxcking way....

62387256

confirmed

so bullish

62387264

the desperation is very real, isn't it?

62387573
>>62387222
an insider schizo visited XSG 1+ years ago and confirmed with digits. buckle up boyz

62387585
Another reason not to go near this train wreck

62387768

OP IS A LIAR GTA VI IS USING HBAR

item.title: The cursor acquisition was the pico-top and it makes perfect sense. — 9 replies — 6.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The cursor acquisition was the pico-top and it makes perfect sense.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-19 12:59 UTC

OP

The cursor acquisition was the pico-top and it makes perfect sense. The median SPCX/TSLA investor does not want to hear about financials or revenue, they want to be sold dreams.

If Elon had just kept talking about the Sun and Mars it'd probably be at $5 trillion right now but using shares to buy some gay AI model shellshocks the goyim back to reality

Early replies (first 3)

62384853
>>62384779
i'm holding on until July once nasdaq fund inclusion is done

62384960
>>62384853
Good luck, I shorted at $211 with crumbs. Will hold that and possibly add leading into the August unlocks if the price is still anywhere near my entry

62385087
>>62384779
Cursor is pretty based though

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62386250
>>62384779
Elon probably heard someone say AI agents are the future and thought "oh sheit I need to buy an AI agent company straight away"

62386353
>>62384853
this literally and someone said spcx will reach 300-350 by the end of july so i'll sell at around that price range. im not holding the august bags fuck that lmoaooo

62387643
>>62385087
Is it $60B based?

62387734
>>62384779
i still can't believe they paid 60 billion for a vscode wrapper
you can just install vscode with copilot

62387742
>>62387734
when musk went shopping around for acquisitions at the picotop, cursor was probably the only company willing to accept fairy dust as payment

62387745
>>62386353
It’ll never reach that. The second insiders can offload their shares they will. The only reason it didn’t nosedive after IPO was lockups

item.title: $260 is still at play — 4 replies — 1.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
$260 is still at play

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-19 12:13 UTC

OP

Just a tiny dip. I'll keep on buying SPCX.

Early replies (first 3)

What if more shares are added you will be fucked.

62386307
>>62386284
>the dumb 5% float FUD again
Wishful thinking. If more shares are added, everyone will just slurp it up anyway so who cares.

62387624
>>62386279
Bold, considering the upcoming unlocks and the $60B all stock deal to acquire Cursor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387652
>>62386279
the armpit smell is clouding your brain

item.title: What made you still hold BTC? — 79 replies — 127.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What made you still hold BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-06-19 13:42 UTC

OP

Bitcoin has gone through multiple cycles, crashes, and endless criticism, yet many continue to hold. What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

Early replies (first 3)

62371733
>>62371718
>What keeps your conviction strong after all these years?

62371807
The only reason to ever sell bitcoin is to buy more at a lower price.

62371917
>>62371807
correct
>waiting for a good entry

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382804
>>62381740
Are you serious nigger?

62382895
buying when boomers capitulate and then riding it all the way to the top must be one of the best feelings ever, but somehow i always end up not buying because I always get lured by bears into waiting for "bettery entry prices".

62383146
>>62382799
Not really. Being a store of value is already a use case. Some people are just interested in whether BTC can be used for more than buying, holding, and eventually selling.

62383366
>>62383146
Where do you do that?

62383375
>>62382895
Just buy today and you will thank in 10 years

62383385
>>62377444
it has

62383588
>>62383366
DYOR on Babylon and you'll see.

62383598
>>62371718
I will still hold it

62383603
>>62382895
How many btc do you hold?

62383619
>>62371718
Sunk cost fallacy. I think I'm retarded. If bitcoin does moon I'm still retarded, but a lucky retard.

62383732
>>62383619
We gonna be rich

62383734
>>62383588
Low apr and kinda not worthy

62383743
>>62375554
Etf is even better

62384166
>>62383734
Sure, it's not life-changing yield, but for some people it's better than letting their BTC sit idle.

62384175
>>62383743
If all you want is price exposure, sure. A lot of people still prefer owning the actual asset.

62384208
Where is satoshi?

62384223
>>62377200
It's all about hype and doesnt have any value

62384235
>>62372468
You better trade memecoins for that

62385618
>>62384235
Go for it. Nobody's stopping you, anon

62386979
>>62384208
Nobody knows

62387122
>>62384166
Do you think it will attract a lot of interest in the long run? The model looks good, and since it's self-custodial while expanding Bitcoin's use cases, I don't think it raises the usual concerns that many people have with DeFi.

62387125
>>62383619
Anon is relaxing

62387534
>>62387122
We'll know for sure once TBV launches on mainnet. That's when we'll see if it gains adoption and makes a real impact.

62387549
>>62383732
If you buy memecoins, not $btc

62387563
>>62387534
Defi is no longer valuable

62387566
Im fine

62387833
>>62387563
Depends what you mean by valuable. A lot of the hype is gone, but people are still borrowing, lending, and farming yield.

62387836
>>62387549
Doge coins lol

62387875
>>62371718
Because it's the only crypto asset I'd be comfortable holding for the next decade without constantly checking the chart.

62387892
the longer this market exists, the more Bitcoin seems to become the benchmark everything else gets compared against. So very very bullish

62379519
>>62377191
tbf I like grok more than gemini, copilot, or deepseek. Not sure yet how I think it stacks up to claude or chatgpt though.

62381795
>>62375140
how to gotted so much dollars?

62381800
>>62375128
Imagine believing 2 weeks represents absolutely anything about the viability or future trajectory of a stock. No wonder you guys get annihilated so regularly.

62381830
>>62377191
SpaceX performs 60% of the world's space launches, which is 95% of the American market. If they aren't a space company, then there isn't a space company that exists at all.

62381991
>>62381830
>He thinks (((outer space))) is like in the movies and what they teach in (((schools))) and show on your (((television)))
Cute. You need to be 18 to post here, champ.

62382075
>>62375140
>>62375255
Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.
While indians are busy generating piles of poo here on earth to jerk off to, real MEN will be mining asteroids in space and speculating on the Asteroid Belt stock exchange

62382118
my parents aped in and told me they didn't want to get left behind lol. Load zee 60% dump.

62382338
>>62382075
>real MEN will be mining asteroids

62382719
>>62382075
>Unlike faggy AI, space is an actual next frontier of human development.

SpaceX won't fucking EXIST by the time we get there.

62382732
>>62382118
That's really sad :(

62382961
>>62379484

62384212
>>62375140
Musk haters BTFO

People fail to realize that it's not just SpaceX. It's X.com, it's xAI, It's X payments. It's literally everything internet related. People can hate on Musk all they want, but he will own the internet and all internet related activity including purchasing, social media will go through his companies. Enjoy staying poor

62384226
>>62384212
>X.com, it's xAI, It's X payments
so it's facebook. although I like xAI, half of their stuff is good on the AI scale but sourcing data from twitter is a mistake.

62384237
>>62384226
It could have been Zuck, but he's straight up unlikable and wasted so much time and money on the wrong tech (VR) while everyone else moved to AI

62384247
Lol @ this thread
Elon is a weird incel

62384277
SpaceKeks

62384281
>>62375128
OP ARE YOU OK??

62384293
kwab

62384467
>>62381830
SpaceX is an AI company. Rockets are a literal hobby when you look at the specifics of the valuation. It’s like buying a house in order to get the Twinkie sitting inside the fridge.

62384596
bought at 170 sold at 203 how low it can go before I slurp back in

62385059
>>62375393
>IF Elon can deliver on what he claims, then this stock is cheap.
Data centers in space arent worth it though. Its massively more expensive and doesnt even make it easier for the user.

There is no space business outside of gov contracts and a few sat companies.

62385083
>>62375128
behold the power of autism and stock manipulation. kek.

62385509
>>62385059
The benefit of data centers in space is that is exponentially increases the cost and technical headaches while heavily limiting the ability of the consumer to utilize it

62387344
>>62385509
Also you can host child porn on them and no jurisdiction can reach you cause you're in orbit

62387424
>>62385509

they need orbital AI because it reduces latency, which is critical for defense; its
much better to have the data center in space when calculating missile interception, for instance.

62387496
Its going to be the same playbook. Pump in the initial first days, dump after the unlocks happen, and slowly creep back up after a years worth of dumping.

62387710
I bought 30 shares, am I gonna make it?

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 78 replies — 15.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-06-19 09:23 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62378002
>>62373918
Well the /ic/ thread got deleted within minutes this is why I am cautious whenever we start to plan or congregate together its like they hate that.

62378439
>>62374533
Photonics sounds solid
I base all of the following off my schizo intuition:

1. I keep hearing about how Elon musk is a retard and SpaceX IPO is a rug pull. But you don't get that rich by being stupid.

2. I can't see X ever being profitable (though it is valuable for other reasons), and nobody gives a shit about electric cars anymore. But SpaceX... he is throwing money on that for a reason.

3. And Starlink is the world's biggest photonics deployment in space right now.
Satellite internet + photonics is going to be a huge deal
- cheap, super fast internet (terabits per second) that doesnt depend on today's bloated over-regulated infrastructure, and you can access it from anywhere
- Governments - specially EUcucks - keep making it difficult for you to use the internet and have free speech, could get BTFO the same way as the twitter trannies did when elon bought X
- Any retard shithole country with underwater drones can cut underwater internet cables, shooting down a satelite would be more difficult
- Perfect opportunity for a psyop to push people into using starlink instead of traditional intenet

If you couple that with AI companies also wanting photonics for their datacenters, you could get a lot of money this way

62378965
Brothers,
Just learn how to use the order book in polymarket, it's full of idiots that are willing to buy your bags for absurd prices. We can set our profile pictures to anime pic rel >62373866 to coordinate, target events like the current fifa event and mass shill or fud a team or group of teams so we can pump our bags
Two days ago i made 4K by just selling Belgium to win the world cup at 3x the actual price and some nigger actually bought it

62379061
>>62377886
Vague platitude

Option number 8 by >>62378965

There could be gains to be made on polymarket. Many of us are American so we'd either use kalshi or a vpn

The anon was so kind to give us an excerpt on his success and /biz/ has yet to make an impact on these markets like it did in 2021 in the BSC casino

By the end of the day, we shall have a decision set

62380691
Bump

62381251
Bump

62381343
>>62378002
/ic/ is filled with schizoids and endless seethe. And this is coming from someone who draws as a hobby.

62381797
>>62373752
>4 figure hell
unironically get a job, any job
like just work a year lol

62383107
It's settled, tune in tomorrow!

62383529
>>62373752
I am all in on SpaceX with my poorstack €6k

62383569
>>62373752
finally,a good general

62384962
Yes, leverage everything and bet it all on the moon shot. You won't regret it.

62384983
Patiently waiting for the hundred bucks of link I bought a decade ago to turn me into a millionaire.

Any day now.

62384990
>>62381797
I AM TRYING
BUT NOBODY IS HIRING

62384995
>>62378002
It's base policy to stop /ic/ from turning into a Patreon beggar spam board

62385082
>>62384990
Use your CPU to mine Nerva/XNV then. A properly decentralized crypto with no mining pools. Even a laptop works!

62385259
I bought 3 shares of spacex at 172 each should I kill myself

62385391
>>62381343
Would you like to join a group of /biz/tards who also took a liking to drawing as a hobby?
>>62384995
Yeah that makes sense, although they do have a dedicated thread to shill their blogs which would conveniently link up to any Patreon.

62385458
>>62385259
why are you chasing hype

62385552
>>62385458
not smart

62385679
>>62373888
Well video games suck ass now. But we have AI which is supposed to help make projects like that easier. Godot is a free open source video game engine that is supposedly easy to use. Why don’t we learn how to do that?

62385716
>>62385391
>Would you like to join a group of /biz/tards who also took a liking to drawing as a hobby?
Sure

62385756
>>62385716
https://archive.4plebs.org/pol/thread/528268175/#528271334

Two links are in the archive one is for shitposting and the other is more art related it's the only way to share them here.

62385759
>>62383107
Ok, so you said it’s settled. What is the decision? Btw if your current mascot doesnt work out I nominate that we use anime Elizabeth Holmes as our mascot. Plus she better exemplifies us anyways.

62385818
34 with 8k to my name
Should I just give up? Honest question

62386248
I have a negative NW of $45k at 31. Unemployed. I don’t know how it all went so wrong for me. I don’t know how to fix it. I have a degree but it doesn’t do shit for me. Worst part is that I had almost $300k 4 years ago lmao.

62386520
>>62386248
How did that happen

62386827
>>62385759
Call me strange but I prefer looking at the real thing.

That being said we'll stick to the two most posted anime girls for the mascot.

62386828
>>62386827
>>62385759

62387408
>>62385259
Just hodl, I also have 4 shares but I also have a nw in 6 figures mainly in index fund and cash interest accounts, I treat it as a mid term to long term gamble. It may be massively overvalued but markets can go full retard for the stupidest of reasons. Just remember to take initial out with some profits if it does.

item.title: AVAX bros... is it fucking over? — 15 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
AVAX bros... is it fucking over?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-19 12:57 UTC

OP

AVAX bros... is it fucking over?

Early replies (first 3)

62384253
>>62384104
Avaxchan will outlive Avax, ironically.

62384835
Don't sully awoo with your shitcoin
Ethereum will outlast all copycats.

62384879
>>62384104

This shitcoin still exists? kek I thought it went the way of ADA, Nano, etc.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384889
It's not over as long as we dont lose hope, god fucking damnit

62387210
it's so bad bros wtf, i'm now 5 years older but poorer than ever

62387214
i rememer when it hit 200 or 100 and you held KEK

62387370
>>62384253
this, but unironically

62387382
Why do they call it Avalanche?

62387431
>>62384104
Fuck this crab dumping piece of trash coin. Going down faster than you can say double spend.

62387461
>>62387370
Thanks for the Avaxchan, I didn't have it. What's hilarious is that I collect Avaxchan images but I don't have a single one. Honestly, Avalanche would be more useful and make more money producing catgirl anime, or even NFTs, rather than maintaining this completely useless zombie blockchain.

62387488
itt: fuckin morons

62387582
Why is it dumping more than everything else? Did something happen?

62387608
>>62384104
I remember entering one of your AVAX generals at ATH and telling all the idiots to sell, of course they didn't listen, thought you were special, now suffer.

62387715
>>62387582
The chain was AI hacked, zec style but nobody wants to pull the rug on baggies. it's fundamentally over.

62387737
>>62384104
it never even began, EVERY SINGLE SHITCOIN that isn't Bitcoin is worthless on a long enough time frame. Show me a company that works in an industry outside of crypto that uses Ethereum, avax, fucking litecoin or near/whatever and it helps make profits. Every coin is a memecoin completely useless to the real world. Instead of complaining that it went down so much you should be thankful that it's still worth anything at all and you can sell to some clueless retard for actual money

item.title: > age 34 > drained down to last $20k — 85 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
> age 34 > drained down to last $20k

Open thread • Replies: 85 • Updated: 2026-06-19 06:16 UTC

OP

> literally impossible to get just a fucking interview these days
> once had $160k nw (circa 2022) but combination of awful timing decisions with crypto + 4 years unemployed did a toll on me with living expenses
> entrepreneurship is hypersaturated beyond belief, impossible to make money online now
> AI is slaughtering every money making field out there so I can't figure out which masters degree or cert I need to go for next and they're all so fucking expensive too

I'm so scared bros HELP

Early replies (first 3)

62382538
>unemployed 4 years
lmao, imagine being this worthless

62382547
>>62382527
just think if you had bought chip stocks instead of fucking around with crypto you would probably be a millionaire.

62382548
>>62382538
fuck off nigger ur probably some 19 year old shithead u dont know anything life will rape u and humble u faggot
>>62382527
Dont worry about it anon im sure u can find a decent job soon

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62384401
>>62382655
It’s like the guy whose girlfriend threw his bitcoin hardrive into the trash and he spent 10 years going through the landfill to try and find it. Nigga, your bags are never going to pump. It’s ok to hold them, just move on and do something else with your life. On the off chance they go up, you still have them, but you are too emotionally invested. This is also a reason you will never sell, too much greed and emotions.

62384614
>>62384309
> durrrhurrr stupid reeeetaaaarrrrdddd JUST TAKE A JOB THAT DOESN'T PAY YOU NEARLY ENOUGH TO GET YOUR OWN APARTMENT AND AFFORD FOOD WITHOUT BLEEDING OUT YOUR SAVINGS HURRRRHURRRHURRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRRR
> NOOOOO BRROOOOOOO YOU'RE SUPPOSED TO START AT THE VERY PITCH BOTTOM AND HAVE MANAGEMENT LIKE YOU ENOUGH TO PROMOTE YOU BROOOOOOOOOO

That aside, this thread has sadly proven biz is long long dead. A few good replies there and there, but most are completely unserious replies like yours.

62384677
>>62384614
Kek because the alternative is not working at all throughout 4 whole years and burning over 100k in savings you fucking ape

62384868
>>62382527
Compile llama.cpp on your computer and learn how to use it, while you get a cert in AI from a community college.

62385517
>>62382527
people shit on trades but they will be some of the only jobs available until robots are able to do them in 50 years

62385532
>>62382527
> literally impossible to get just a fucking interview these days
Just apply at some jobs that require no experience. Physical labor ones are always hiring. Live in a small apartment until you find a higher paying job. Problem solved.

62385962
>>62385532
If you do that though, you have to make sure your resume doesn’t indicate you’ll be looking for a better job

62385968
>>62384309
> everywhere has the same opportunity as my area
Who’s the retard?

62385969
>>62385532
This is real
>just stop buying avocado toasts
tier bullshit

62385982
>>62382527
you seem perfect for construction, retail and blue collar work!

62385999
>>62383084
Yes, but you need money to make more money. Only a NEET would avoid getting a job at all costs.

62386061
>>62382527
Setup a homeschooling service where you teach kids how to do basic programming/ use AI tools/ learn how circuitry works for 3 hours 1 day a week. Charge $300 per kid a month with a minimum of a 1 semester commitment. 20 kids = $6,000 a month.

62386091
>>62382527
Go to an auction and buy a skid steer for $20,000 and start a land management business.

62386110
>>62383334
How much chinese do you need to know for a job like this? Do you work fully remote? How did you start? I've been taking chinese lessons but still very new to the language.

62386120
>>62385969
Explain how I'm wrong. OP is bitching about not being able to get a job which is fucking absurd considering there are places that are always hiring. Yes the pay at those places is usually bad but it's better than nothing while you continue to look for a better one. You can afford to live to live almost anywhere as long as you live below your means.

62386126
>>62386120
Have you considered the price of even the most fucked up roach infested 20 jeet shared room lately?
>Back in my days 1 latte paid for the mortgage. Kids these days...

62386231
>>62382627
Yep I'm a grade IV with only an associates. I just got my restricted before applying for jobs with zero experience and within 4 years I'm making 34$/hr.

62386247
>>62386120
Not every job is actually worth your labor. Keep your self respect up bro, you’ll make it

62386262
>>62382527
USA Is a country for losers. Get out and find a better life.

62386267
Safe index.

62386343
I'm ugly because of shit genetics so I should be paid a disability check it's literally the same thing as having a disability.

It has negatively affected my life to where it has prevented me from achieving success therefore I should be subsidized.

Why do people with autism get disability, and btw I have both but I never claimed autism was my downfall. Being ugly is 10x worse you can't even work a menial job because zoomies will make fun of you

62386365
>>62386343
bullshit Im 10x uglier and command respect and always make friends and partnerships because I act chadlike. yur issue is not your face its in your fucking brain.

62386369
>>62386365
Nta but there is a point of ugliness that society is not cool with regardless of attitude.

62386425
>>62384161
Imagine how easy you would end up employed if you were a woman

62386498
>>62382527

Viewpoint214.com

is for you and anyone else who needs help...

All you have to do is enter your name and email address.

No scams or ponzi schemes. 100% legit.

62386569
>>62386343

>Downfall

It's downSIDE you fucking retard. Get a job, invest, and stop gambling on retard-tier stocks. 20k in NLCP would get you $2400/year for sitting on your ass. Well, maybe 2200 now, idgaf I bought when it was yielding 15% and I've gotten banger dividends with mild capital appreciation and almost zero risk.

Job = living expenses. Invest in a few solid companies. Don't buy hype bullshit. Don't by hot sectors. By good companies in sectors nobody gives a shit about, like Cannabis, Agriculture, REITs, etc. Don't buy any company unless it has rock-solid financials.

And stop saying downfalls you fucking faggot this isn't an Anime.

62386574
>>62386425
Am i too ugly to be employed as a woman? Can’t find a job either

62387163
>>62386369
ive never matched or dated anyone I am genuinely disfigured. virgin at 40. but you learn to let that go and focus on the things that do work and things you do enjoy. I aint finna let ugliness cramp my style. Used to in the past (20+yrs ago). Waste of time

62387166
>>62387163
ofc there are cretins below even me i attest that. but if you saw me youd agree Im probably like in the bottom 0.1% or worse

62387185
when everything goes tits up i'll eat all of you.

item.title: >he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family? — 18 replies — 9.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-19 12:27 UTC

OP

>he went all in on everything except the only investment that matters

Why didn't YOU invest in your family?

Early replies (first 3)

62379163
you're asking why I didn't invest in my family...

and I will ask in return...

Is killing niggers free and open-source?

62379166
I personally view having kids an essential life goal.
Just give me more time.

62379173
>>62379160
I'm 29 with $300k I'm gonna get my gf pregnant at 30. Hopefully I'll have 400k by the

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62379191
>>62379160
I'm trying, bro.

62379204
>>62379166
>I just need to complete this massive list of things that likely won't happen before I start a family

the equivalent of waiting for a big price dip in a good stock that you subconsciously know isn't going to happen

62379219
>>62379160
Bad ROI and infinite P/E.

62379223
>>62379204
Having kids is like being a cuck in a relationship. You watch from the sidelines as others fullfill their lives while you wait your sloppy seconds.

62379248
>>62379223
Lol

62379259
Next bullrun when I'm 40-something I'll find a prime 25 year old girl to marry and have children with.

It's not too late r-right?

62383378
>>62379259
No, but you'll have to accept that she only likes you for money

62383390
>>62379259
You are an old hag, it is so creepy

62383494
>>62379160
What makes you think I didn't?

62383537
>>62379160
You might be able to get a submissive 25yo trad chink wife lining up for you in year 2000 to year 2010 where chink is literally a sinkhole stone age tarzan living in the cave or on top of the tree, that have zero exposure to internet other than brick games. Today you would be lucky to get a brown shitskin 25yo to want to talk to you.

62387425
>>62379160
>Why didn't YOU invest in your family?
Simple, bad ROI

62387541
>>62379160
>have sex incel
>biz edish

62387661
>>62379160

62387677
>>62379223
This sounds like waging, lol

62387680

item.title: how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE… — 80 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEE…

Open thread • Replies: 80 • Updated: 2026-06-19 02:42 UTC

OP

how much money do u need to date women? Also are they ok if u are not ambitious career driven like if i have 10M but NEET would they be fine with it.. probably not i think they want you to be grinding your job 24/7 and making like 400k a year

Early replies (first 3)

62365550
dating is outdated chud, find something else to focus on

62365555
>>62365550
but i want a gf to watch mobis with, to eat some delicious food, cook together maybe and stuff.. I got a bluray player recently and im building up a collection we could do it together if i had a gf

:(

62365557
>>62365540
a good prostitute in euro cuck land will service you starting from 100$ for 30 mins. Anything else is cope

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369807
>>62365540
Elon is worth $1,000,000,000,000 and still can't find a woman that wants to stay with him

62371591
>>62369133
> take shrooms
Based

>>62368990
You’re letting your insecurities delude you.

62371912
>>62365555
you sound like a retard

62371972
>>62369133
>Also girls can have guy friends that they don't wanna fuck.
even if this is the case the guy most likely wants to fuck them you retard.

part of being in a relationship is having a basic level of respect for your partner. that means dropping all single behaviour, so no hanging out 1 on 1 with the opposite sex, no texting other guys, and no getting drunk without you in bars/clubs. its not insecurity to expect a the most basic of relationship boundaries.

>just take drugs bro!
>go pay money to a millenial woman
holy bluepilled retard

62373795
>>62369807
tbf net worth is not how much money you have

62374837
>>62371972
You're correct that having boundaries isn't insecurity, but not trying to ask your friends if they know a single girl because you believe she talked to your friend is deeply insecure. No one here was talking about behavior after meeting, this dude won't even want to speak to a girl that would've known a male friend
NGMI behavior

62378030
>>62365555
What is up with normies and "cooking"??? I like preparing my own fucking meals and I don't want to cook with you nor eat your food nor make some big show about THE DINNER TABLE NARF NARF NARF like food should be strictly for utilitarian purposes only. Also women have a sinful relationship with food. You would think it was porn to them. Foodies and fat people gross me out. Humans have to ruin so many things. Don't even get me started on BLACK PEOPLE

62378038
>>62365608
Mainly non brown chicks right?

62378415
>>62365540
You have been priced out of prime young teen. Start dating older woman which is closer to the post wall in youth.

62378662
>>62365555
>got a blu ray player
Why not just get on PTP instead of wasting your money?
>>62365595
How old were you?
>>62365989
Holy fuck. I will remain khhv forever
>>62369226

>>62369807
He had a threesome with Cara Delivigne and Amber heard, got prime Amber to do a high quality cosplay and roles of his waifu (mercy) for him, and made prime alt princess grimes completely obsessed with him. He won multiple times over when it comes to women, especially compared to other billionaires.

62378677
>>62378662
>How old were you?
32

62378686
>>62378677
>32
So i still have time, like 2 years away. $800k net. I'm pretty sure I'll hit 1m by then but not so sure about the gf.

62378720
>>62378686
you just launched a whole interdimensional TED Talk powered by three screenshots, two podcasts, and a gas-station energy drink.
You're standing on a folding chair yelling, "EVERY SINGLE WOMAN CAN JUST BECOME RICH, DATE A CELEBRITY, FIND A SUGAR DADDY, OR MARRY SOME DEVOTED GUY," like life is a character-select screen in a video game where everybody spawns with max charisma and infinite social options.
My brother in chaos, that's not how reality works.
You took a handful of visible examples, put them in a blender, added internet rage, and came out with a theory that somehow explains half the human population. That's clown astronomy. You're looking at three bright stars and declaring you've mapped the entire galaxy.
And then when someone tells a story about a rude interaction on a dating app, your brain teleports straight back to the same speech. Like the conversation took a bus stop and you arrived riding a shopping cart with no wheels.
The wild part is that your argument assumes every person has equal opportunities, equal looks, equal luck, equal social skills, equal location, equal connections, and equal willingness to do the things you're listing. That's a level of simplification so aggressive it could flatten a mountain into a parking lot.
Reality is messy. Some people struggle. Some people succeed. Some get lucky. Some don't. Some are attractive and still miserable. Some are average and find great relationships. Human lives aren't assembled from four preset buttons.
So when you scream "EVERY SINGLE ONE," that's the clown horn giving itself CPR.
The issue isn't that you're making a point. The issue is that you're treating a giant, complicated reality like a meme template and acting surprised when people look confused.
You're not describing the world.
You're describing a cartoon version of the world that lives rent-free inside your own echo chamber.

62378730
>>62378720
Ok chatgpt very cool

62378739
>>62366590
Those families are pre 2025+ hypergamy

62378802
>>62366052
>he thinks the average foid has less rates of herpes
lol
lmao

At least high class escorts routinely get checked. You think the average woman's ego would allow her to get regular checks? We're literally at the stage where high class, well-regulated services are safer than your typical bar hookup.

62379944
>>62365621
can you explain to me what a "pickme" is?
I've seen youtube shorts but I still don't understand because it's girls who wear hats and shorts and go to sports bars.
>>62365555
>nothing about sexo
that's a friend not a girlfriend.

62379954
>>62365555
What your looking for is a boyfriend. Straight men do drugs jack off and play video games

62379965
>>62365621
Same man. I've never had a real conversation with a girl save one cool chick in college I dated.

They don't like deep intellectual discussions or pondering the nature of how something works

62379978
>>62379944
>wow I totally love video games
>omg I'm a huge Warhammer 40k fan
Look inside: regular chick with passing interest in the stuff you like
Pick me is pretty self explanatory, they want to be 'picked.
If anything sports chicks are more likely to turn on a game and want to watch in my experience but sports are kinda gay

62380391
>>62365555
Yeah sorry buddy, you need to be 20 in the early 2000s for that life, now it is only fans and thotting around for the next 50 years. Women are more susceptible to the psychic social media poison

62380855
>>62379978
weird but I think I've met a few. they always seemed fake

62382230
>>62365540
Being broke works the best for me personally

62385537
>>62365626
Ironic that Henry looks better than you, but came to the conclusion life is better settling with a basic girl and staying at home playing video games

62385567
>>62365748
this. Even if you marry a gold digger, it's not fulfilling. It's why Bill Gates etc just stick to 10/10 escorts ordered right to their hotels discretely and the occasional island trips.

>>62369807
see above. Obviously he can, but there's no point in legally marrying someone who's poorer than you. He's also too degen. He was literally trying to bring his ex wife to the Epstein island thing with him when they were still together. I think he can't relate to any girls unless they're really fucked in the head like Grimes and even she couldn't stand his work schedule after a couple years.

>>62378415
yeah, if not attractive enough to fuck/marry them in middle/high school, it's over. Either settle with someone nice and helpful or/and rent chicks out for the hour.

62385595
>>62365540
Invest in my startup. Bitches love investors.

62385960
>>62380855
Because they are, they’re putting on a front to be picked instead of being themselves. Like that red headed pearl lady

62386790
>>62385960
who?

62386805
Money is the access to places, things. Got my first gf broke. You clowns dont know how to RIZZ A WOMAN LOLOLOL show her your cock be a man.

62386818
>>62365540
>>62365540
at least $100k salary

item.title: Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology — 68 replies — 6.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investing in Sex Robots & Aritifical Womb Technology

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-06-19 04:23 UTC

OP

This will be one of the greatest investment opportunities in our lifetime within another decade or so.

Tech is stagnating, just as every other industry is out of viable optimization methodologies. There is nothing new being created, every niche is saturated and optimized. AI will produce some value but it is ultimately a giant bubble that will never, and I mean never advance to the point of consciousness or intelligence (unless they use biological brains and do so correctly - they will fail). Science doesn't even know where consciousness comes.

That being said: sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness) will be the big new technology that solves real world problems. And the funny thing is: tech companies will eventually run out of other means of generating increasing revenue and will essentially HAVE to go this route, just like they're currently doing with AI. They have nothing else. This technology will have vast and continuous demand as it will entirely replace the old/current paradigm.

Early replies (first 3)

62375864
>>62375553
I want sexbots to be real so bad

62375914
>>62375864
same
all the despair, pain and unhappiness i feel... if it was a good sexbot i would be happy.
the thing is, the first sexbots will be bad. like truly awful. probably not physically dangerous... maybe. but not enjoyable. like how electric masturbators are really good at making me nut, but a soulless experience that leaves me feeling empty. the technology to replace a living organism with a robot is just not there.
if it were viable, there would be news. most people who are lonely and horny want the sexbot. but porn and masturbators are the best we have. sexbots are an AI complete problem.

62375977
>>62375553
>sex robots and eventual artificial womb tech (much easier than actually creating consciousness)
AI is not about creating consciousness. you are retarded if you think intelligence requires consciousness.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62382450
>>62379611
yes but greeks didn't have technology that is buck breaking us.

women are natural chaotic evil. they exemplify the bestial nature of man.

men on the other hand are a hive intelligence. look at this place, we are already a hive mind here. this is the natural state male minds gravitate towards. necessity for women for sustenance kept men away from something like this, until now. but the future will not be like the things you know.

62382453
>>62377166
Son, for all the bad schizo days you know there are also those days of pure awesome mania...

62382457
>>62376404
They'll be fit only as servitors

62382461
>>62375553
>>62376084
>>62379333
>>62380759
>>62380948
op stfu and just give me the public company to ape into

62382474
>artificial womb

why do you want to be a single parent?

62382691
>>62382461
>op stfu
well now I'm not gonna

62382737
>>62382474
because no woman wants anon. no matter how much money anon makes.

62382774
>>62378763
>Women would still be mothers
Across all the AIslop you generated ITT I just had to laugh at this being a "point" made in a discussion where women would, in fact, no longer be mothers.

62382783
>>62382474
>>62382737
uh, what? I'm talking about an investing opportunity on the business board. I actually just got a message a few minutes ago on fb messenger from one of my sisters friends who's been trying to get with me for a year and my night feels ruined now because it was extremely annoying trying to reject her nicely on the basis of "trust me you do not want anything to do with me, I am autistic and do not like leaving the house" and I still had to ghost her completely. I don't date or have any desire to and I don't want to have kids at all. I am extremely handsome but also an autistic freak who... doesn't like to leave the house kek dating is not for me, I am for looking at only.

62382794
>>62382783
Can I look at your feet?

62382802
>>62382794
no thats gross and gay

62382809
thoughts?

62382812
>>62382802
You're for looking only and you won't even let us look? Fucking prude.

62382814
>>62382774
> new tech would be easily available to public and not only accessible by the wealthy
> the poors and religious fundamentalists wouldn’t continue to multiply like rabbits
Yeah, no more mothers, for sure, good point

62382816
>>62382809
That’s an AI video and very creepy

62382832
>>62376404
Truly horror. The audacity of these humans. It's not just humans they perform horrific and abominable procedures too but animals as well.

>>62377063
Trvke. A horror experiment from decades ago where they removed any sort of affection to a batch of babies. Just mechanical feeding via picking up the kid, no affection, and using a bottle. They quickly started dying off and the experiment ended.

62382834
>>62382814
In the context of "women would still hold their value leveraging this to gain more freedom" and using that as a suggestion of how such women would still hold value...

Yeah nah, the women "leveraging" this to obtain more "freedom" would not be mothers. That wouldn't be the go to source of value to anyone coherent trying to argue birthing was irrelevant to a woman's value. It's ultra retarded.

62382837
>>62375914
Just go somewhere you can hire a prostitute you fucking retard. Then when you realize pussy isn't that special, just buy a quality flesh light and move on with your life.
>>62376404
Thanks Anon, I fully intend to make an entire army of white children this way and your diatribe has made sure I will fight for access during incubation.

62382852
Some of you guys are so fucking stupid that you can not see what is happening right in front of you.

We are long past jerking each other off about the ethics and morality of things....if those ideas had any meaning, we would not allow girls to make more money than a fucking surgeon just by selling her bath water.

OP is fucking right - Robo pussy & artifical wombs is going to fucking send.

This is bitcoin when it was $12.76
This is Amazon when jeff was still living in new mexico selling books
This is Youtube when it had like only 1000 accounts that have registered on the site

I just had an old 55+ year old guy tell me the only reason women have value today is because men want whats between there legs. Throw that out and she's not worth the salt.

The world economic forum stated way back almost a decade ago THAT THIS ALONG WITH GENE-DNA EDITING is going to fucking send.

Think you dumb fuck "If robo pussy is bitcoin at $12.76....what does robo-pussy look like when its at a trillion dollar marketcap?"

62382854
>>62382834
I really want to know what woman ever saw motherhood as a path to freedom. Marriage, yes, but motherhood no.
It’s a desire built into them to want concern, similar to men, but freedom has nothing to do with it. Most women recognize the immediate responsibility and shackling to the child, it’s why motherhood age has increased.

The same women who are trophy wives would continue to be trophy wives because their value was never motherhood.
Haha these threads never cease to crack me up.
Where is this misconception that women lorded babies over men’s heads, and not men lording babies over other men’s heads? They’re the keepers of the babies or some shit not because or an avenue to freedom but because if they pick wrong it’s guaranteed misery

62383573
>>62375553
okay and what stock do I invest in?

62383663
>>62376404
get real, we live in a world where it's legal to kill babies and people call them parasites, trash parents with substance abuse issues poisoning kids before they're even born, most animals are better parents than human mothers, this will be an improvement.

62383804
>>62382448
>when hookers are literally right there and are real women
That's kind of the problem though. They're real women. With everything that goes with that, both good and bad. There's definitely demand for something between porn and sex robots, but I also wonder if there isn't something way more immersive and easier than sex robots possible to deliver. Maybe some type of VR experience, short of having to give Elon Musk control of your brain.

62385644
>>62382448
Depends where you live. In a lot of first world countries hooker are illegal and the ones you'll find are either ugly 40s chicks or they request $800 min for half an hour with them.

62386435
>>62375553
This is a bad idea because governments across the world already have laws coming down the pipe banning these incredibly dystopian technologies.

62386545
>>62375553
Sex robot is my hand. My artificial womb is my hand. I don't need to buy poorly made overpriced plastic shit made by jews to cope with my lonelyness

62386576
>>62386545

>Lonely
>Can't afford hookers
>Blames Jews

62386645
>>62386576
I can afford your mom but I grew tired of prostitutes over the years. Too much hassle, choosing a girl, making an appointment, get to her place, only to see a slampig whore reeking of pizza, fuck that. I'd rather watch some amy rose futa banging on rouge the bat and be done with my urges

62386707
>>62375553
I think you’re directionally correct but wrong about the details. I don’t see a huge market for artificial wombs in isolation, it’s not a lack of wombs that are leading to our societal problems, it’s that women as a whole are retards when it comes to shepherding their most valuable commodity: their eggs. So we would need an adjunct tech like IVG for our species to really transcend past a primitive state.

62387048
This thread is a great example of why /biz/ will never make it.

Sexbots/AI romantic companions are already a thing, and its a growing muliti-billion dollar industry. The sexdoll industry is also growing and is highly competitive on the higher end. One of the few cases where AI might actually be what its hyped up to be. All attraction is "coded", whether by evolution or by actual code.

Artificial wombs directly target the growing surrogacy market, where even in cheap jurisdictions like Columbia, the average surrogate agency charges $80k or more. US is more like $200k. This is prime territory for an artificial replacement. The single dad question is already being answered by the surrogacy industry, and their is increasing YoY demand from single men to become fathers.

What I would need to see is some kind of local AI capable of running the bot's mind and the body. It will likely cost $50k in about 10 years just to build the brain that can run the bot and run the brain. The bot will be expensive, but it could be financed on a 10 year term like lots of other luxury goods like exotic cars, boats and other shit. Or it can be part of a worker's compensation package for working in a remote environment like the Arctic for long periods of time.

62386472

62387474
